records.chapter: Chapter on Categories Upon Whom Zakat is Obligatory
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1622 - Ali ibn Ja'far asked his brother Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as) about a man who gives his Zakat for dirhams in the form of dinars, and for dinars in the form of dirhams, based on their value.
Imam (as) replied: "There is no issue with that."
records.arabicText: 1622 - وَ سَأَلَ عَلِيُّ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ أَخَاهُ مُوسَى بْنَ جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ : عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يُعْطِي زَكَاتَهُ عَنِ اَلدَّرَاهِمِ دَنَانِيرَ وَ عَنِ اَلدَّنَانِيرِ دَرَاهِمَ بِالْقِيمَةِ أَ يَحِلُّ ذَلِكَ قَالَ «لاَ بَأْسَ بِهِ».

records.chapter: Chapter on Categories Upon Whom Zakat is Obligatory
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1623 - Muhammad ibn Khalid al-Barqi wrote to Abu Ja'far al-Thani, Imam Muhammad al-Jawad (as) asking:
"Is it permissible to pay the Zakat due on crops such as wheat and barley, or on gold, in dirhams equal to their value, or is it obligatory to pay the Zakat in kind from each category?"
Imam (as) replied: "Whichever is easier may be given."
records.arabicText: 1623 - وَ كَتَبَ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ خَالِدٍ اَلْبَرْقِيُّ إِلَى أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ اَلثَّانِي عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : هَلْ يَجُوزُ أَنْ يُخْرَجَ عَمَّا يَجِبُ فِي اَلْحَرْثِ مِنَ اَلْحِنْطَةِ وَ اَلشَّعِيرِ وَ مَا يَجِبُ عَلَى اَلذَّهَبِ دَرَاهِمُ بِقِيمَةِ مَا يَسْوَى أَمْ لاَ يَجُوزُ إِلاَّ أَنْ يُخْرَجَ مِنْ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ مِمَّا فِيهِ فَأَجَابَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ «أَيُّمَا تَيَسَّرَ يُخْرَجُ».

records.chapter: Chapter on Categories Upon Whom Zakat is Obligatory
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1624 - Umar ibn Yazid asked Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) about a man who evaded paying Zakat on his wealth by purchasing land or a house with it.
He asked: "Is anything still due on it?"
The Imam (as) replied: "No, even if he turned it into jewellery or silver nuggets, nothing is due on it. However, by withholding what he owed to Allah (swt), he deprived himself of a greater blessing than what he withheld from Allah’s (swt) right."
records.arabicText: 1624 - وَ سَأَلَ عُمَرُ بْنُ يَزِيدَ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : عَنْ رَجُلٍ فَرَّ بِمَالِهِ مِنَ اَلزَّكَاةِ فَاشْتَرَى بِهِ أَرْضاً أَوْ دَاراً أَ عَلَيْهِ فِيهِ شَيْءٌ فَقَالَ «لاَ وَ لَوْ جَعَلَهُ حُلِيّاً أَوْ نُقَراً فَلاَ شَيْءَ عَلَيْهِ وَ مَا مَنَعَ نَفْسَهُ مِنْ فَضْلِهِ فَهُوَ أَكْثَرُ مِمَّا مَنَعَ مِنْ حَقِّ اَللَّهِ اَلَّذِي يَكُونُ فِيهِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.1624 - Umar ibn Yazid asked Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) about a man who evaded paying Zakat on his wealth by purchasing land or a house with it.
He asked:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"Is anything still due on it?"
The Imam (as) replied: "No, even if he turned it into jewellery or silver nuggets, nothing is due on it. However, by withholding what he owed to Allah (swt), he deprived himself of a greater blessing than what he withheld from Allah’s (swt) right."

records.chapter: Chapter on Categories Upon Whom Zakat is Obligatory
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1625 - Zurara and Muhammad ibn Muslim narrated from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) who said:
"Any man who possesses wealth, and a year passes over it, must pay Zakat on it."
He was asked: "What if he gifts it away a month or a day before the year completes?"
Imam (as) replied: "Then there is nothing obligatory upon him."
Zurara also narrated from him that he said:
"This is similar to a man who breaks his fast on a day in Month of Ramadan while residing at home, then sets out on a journey later that day with the intention of nullifying the expiation that had become obligatory upon him."
records.arabicText: 1625 - وَ رَوَى زُرَارَةُ وَ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: «أَيُّمَا رَجُلٍ كَانَ لَهُ مَالٌ وَ حَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلْحَوْلُ فَإِنَّهُ يُزَكِّيهِ» قِيلَ لَهُ فَإِنْ وَهَبَهُ قَبْلَ حَوْلِهِ بِشَهْرٍ أَوْ بِيَوْمٍ قَالَ «لَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ شَيْءٌ إِذاً» وَ رَوَى زُرَارَةُ عَنْهُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ «إِنَّمَا هَذَا بِمَنْزِلَةِ رَجُلٍ أَفْطَرَ فِي شَهْرِ رَمَضَانَ يَوْماً فِي إِقَامَتِهِ ثُمَّ يَخْرُجُ فِي آخِرِ اَلنَّهَارِ فِي سَفَرٍ وَ أَرَادَ بِسَفَرِهِ ذَلِكَ إِبْطَالَ اَلْكَفَّارَةِ اَلَّتِي وَجَبَتْ عَلَيْهِ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1625 - Zurara and Muhammad ibn Muslim narrated from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"Any man who possesses wealth, and a year passes over it, must pay Zakat on it."
He was asked: "What if he gifts it away a month or a day before the year completes?"
Imam (as) replied: "Then there is nothing obligatory upon him."
Zurara also narrated from him that he said:
"This is similar to a man who breaks his fast on a day in Month of Ramadan while residing at home, then sets out on a journey later that day with the intention of nullifying the expiation that had become obligatory upon him."

records.chapter: Chapter on Categories Upon Whom Zakat is Obligatory
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1626 - Abu Ja’far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) said regarding the nine categories (liable for Zakat):
"If they are converted (to another form) within the year, then nothing is due on them."
records.arabicText: 1626 - وَ قَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي اَلتِّسْعَةِ اَلْأَصْنَافِ «إِذَا حَوَّلْتَهَا فِي اَلسَّنَةِ فَلَيْسَ عَلَيْكَ فِيهَا شَيْءٌ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1626 - Abu Ja’far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) said regarding the nine categories (liable for Zakat):
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If they are converted (to another form) within the year, then nothing is due on them."

records.chapter: Chapter on Categories Upon Whom Zakat is Obligatory
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1627 - Abu Ja'far (Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir) and Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) were asked about a man who owns a house, a servant, and a slave. "Can he accept Zakat?"
They (as) replied: "Yes, because the house and the servant are not considered wealth."
records.arabicText: 1627 - وَ سُئِلَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ وَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ : عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ لَهُ دَارٌ وَ خَادِمٌ وَ عَبْدٌ أَ يَقْبَلُ اَلزَّكَاةَ قَالاَ «نَعَمْ إِنَّ اَلدَّارَ وَ اَلْخَادِمَ لَيْسَا بِمَالٍ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.1627 - Abu Ja'far (Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir) and Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) were asked about a man who owns a house, a servant, and a slave. "Can he accept Zakat?"
They (as) replied:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Yes, because the house and the servant are not considered wealth."

records.chapter: Chapter on Categories Upon Whom Zakat is Obligatory
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1628 - It is permissible for a man who owns seven hundred (dirhams or similar) to accept Zakat, while it is prohibited for a man who owns fifty. This is because the one with seven hundred may have many dependents, and if he divides his wealth among them, it will not suffice.
In such a case, he should abstain from taking it for himself but may take it for his dependents.
As for the one with fifty, it is prohibited for him to accept Zakat if he is alone and works in a profession through which he earns enough to suffice himself, by Allah’s (swt) will.
records.arabicText: 1628 - وَ قَدْ تَحِلُّ اَلزَّكَاةُ لِصَاحِبِ اَلسَّبْعِمِائَةٍ وَ تَحْرُمُ عَلَى صَاحِبِ اَلْخَمْسِينَ إِذَا كَانَ صَاحِبُ اَلسَّبْعِمِائَةٍ لَهُ عِيَالٌ كَثِيرٌ فَلَوْ قَسَمَهَا بَيْنَهُمْ لَمْ تَكْفِهِ فَلْيُعِفَّ عَنْهَا نَفْسَهُ وَ لْيَأْخُذْهَا لِعِيَالِهِ وَ أَمَّا صَاحِبُ اَلْخَمْسِينَ فَإِنَّهُ تَحْرُمُ عَلَيْهِ إِذَا كَانَ وَحْدَهُ وَ هُوَ مُحْتَرِفٌ يَعْمَلُ بِهَا وَ هُوَ يُصِيبُ فِيهَا مَا يَكْفِيهِ إِنْ شَاءَ اَللَّهُ تَعَالَى».

records.chapter: Chapter on Categories Upon Whom Zakat is Obligatory
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1629 - Sama'ah narrated from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) who said:
I asked him about Zakat: "Is it permissible for a man who owns a house and a servant to accept Zakat?"
Imam (as) replied: "Yes, unless his house is a source of income (dar ghallah) from which he receives enough income to suffice himself and his dependents. If the income from it does not suffice for their food, clothing, and needs without extravagance, then it is permissible for him to accept Zakat. However, if its income suffices them, then it is not permissible."
records.arabicText: 1629 - وَ رَوَى سَمَاعَةُ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ اَلزَّكَاةِ هَلْ تَصْلُحُ لِصَاحِبِ اَلدَّارِ وَ اَلْخَادِمِ فَقَالَ «نَعَمْ إِلاَّ أَنْ تَكُونَ دَارُهُ دَارَ غَلَّةٍ فَيَدْخُلُ لَهُ مِنْ غَلَّتِهَا ، مَا يَكْفِيهِ لِنَفْسِهِ وَ عِيَالِهِ فَإِنْ لَمْ تَكُنِ اَلْغَلَّةُ تَكْفِيهِ لِنَفْسِهِ وَ عِيَالِهِ فِي طَعَامِهِمْ وَ كِسْوَتِهِمْ وَ حَاجَتِهِمْ فِي غَيْرِ إِسْرَافٍ فَقَدْ حَلَّتْ لَهُ اَلزَّكَاةُ وَ إِنْ كَانَتْ غَلَّتُهَا تَكْفِيهِمْ فَلاَ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1629 - Sama'ah narrated from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
I asked him about Zakat: "Is it permissible for a man who owns a house and a servant to accept Zakat?"
Imam (as) replied: "Yes, unless his house is a source of income (dar ghallah) from which he receives enough income to suffice himself and his dependents. If the income from it does not suffice for their food, clothing, and needs without extravagance, then it is permissible for him to accept Zakat. However, if its income suffices them, then it is not permissible."

records.chapter: Chapter on Categories Upon Whom Zakat is Obligatory
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1630 - Abu Basir asked Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) about a man who possesses eight hundred dirhams, works as a cobbler, and has many dependents:
"Is he allowed to take Zakat?"
The Imam (as) replied: "O Abu Muhammad, does he profit from his dirhams enough to sustain his dependents and still have a surplus?"
Abu Basir said: "Yes."
The Imam asked: "How much surplus does he have?"
Abu Basir replied: "I do not know."
The Imam (as) said: "If the surplus is equivalent to half of their sustenance, then he should not take Zakat. But if it is less than half, then he may take Zakat."
Abu Basir then asked: "Does he have to pay Zakat on his wealth?"
The Imam (as) replied: "Yes."
Abu Basir asked: "What should he do?"
The Imam (as) said: "He should use it to provide more generously for his dependents in their food and clothing and keep a portion to give to others. Whatever he takes from Zakat, he should use to improve the condition of his dependents until they reach a standard comparable to others."
records.arabicText: 1630 - وَ سَأَلَ أَبُو بَصِيرٍ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يَكُونُ لَهُ ثَمَانُمِائَةِ دِرْهَمٍ وَ هُوَ رَجُلٌ خَفَّافٌ وَ لَهُ عِيَالٌ كَثِيرٌ أَ لَهُ أَنْ يَأْخُذَ مِنَ اَلزَّكَاةِ فَقَالَ «يَا أَبَا مُحَمَّدٍ أَ يَرْبَحُ فِي دَرَاهِمِهِ مَا يَقُوتُ بِهِ عِيَالَهُ وَ يَفْضُلُ» قَالَ نَعَمْ قَالَ «كَمْ يَفْضُلُ» قَالَ لاَ أَدْرِي قَالَ «إِنْ كَانَ يَفْضُلُ عَنِ اَلْقُوتِ مِقْدَارُ نِصْفِ اَلْقُوتِ فَلاَ يَأْخُذِ اَلزَّكَاةَ وَ إِنْ كَانَ أَقَلَّ مِنْ نِصْفِ اَلْقُوتِ أَخَذَ اَلزَّكَاةَ» قَالَ قُلْتُ فَعَلَيْهِ فِي مَالِهِ زَكَاةٌ تَلْزَمُهُ قَالَ «بَلَى» قَالَ قُلْتُ كَيْفَ يَصْنَعُ قَالَ «يُوَسِّعُ بِهَا عَلَى عِيَالِهِ فِي طَعَامِهِمْ وَ كِسْوَتِهِمْ وَ يُبْقِي مِنْهَا شَيْئاً يُنَاوِلُهُ غَيْرَهُمْ وَ مَا أَخَذَ مِنَ اَلزَّكَاةِ فَضَّهُ عَلَى عِيَالِهِ حَتَّى يُلْحِقَهُمْ بِالنَّاسِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1630 - Abu Basir asked Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) about a man who possesses eight hundred dirhams, works as a cobbler, and has many dependents:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"Is he allowed to take Zakat?"
The Imam (as) replied: "O Abu Muhammad, does he profit from his dirhams enough to sustain his dependents and still have a surplus?"
Abu Basir said: "Yes."
The Imam asked: "How much surplus does he have?"
Abu Basir replied: "I do not know."
The Imam (as) said: "If the surplus is equivalent to half of their sustenance, then he should not take Zakat. But if it is less than half, then he may take Zakat."
Abu Basir then asked: "Does he have to pay Zakat on his wealth?"
The Imam (as) replied: "Yes."
Abu Basir asked: "What should he do?"
The Imam (as) said: "He should use it to provide more generously for his dependents in their food and clothing and keep a portion to give to others. Whatever he takes from Zakat, he should use to improve the condition of his dependents until they reach a standard comparable to others."

records.chapter: Chapter on Categories Upon Whom Zakat is Obligatory
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1631 - Abdullah ibn Ajlan al-Sakuni said to Abu Ja'far (Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as)):
"Sometimes I distribute something among my companions to help them. How should I give it to them?"
The Imam (as) replied: "Give it to them based on their commitment to the faith, understanding of religion, and intellect."
records.arabicText: 1631 - وَ قَالَ عَبْدُ اَللَّهِ بْنُ عَجْلاَنَ اَلسَّكُونِيُّ لِأَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : إِنِّي رُبَّمَا قَسَمْتُ اَلشَّيْءَ بَيْنَ أَصْحَابِي أَصِلُهُمْ بِهِ فَكَيْفَ أُعْطِيهِمْ فَقَالَ «أَعْطِهِمْ عَلَى اَلْهِجْرَةِ فِي اَلدِّينِ وَ اَلْفِقْهِ وَ اَلْعَقْلِ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1631 - Abdullah ibn Ajlan al-Sakuni said to Abu Ja'far (Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as)):
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"Sometimes I distribute something among my companions to help them. How should I give it to them?"
The Imam (as) replied: "Give it to them based on their commitment to the faith, understanding of religion, and intellect."

records.chapter: Chapter on Categories Upon Whom Zakat is Obligatory
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1632 - Muhammad ibn Muslim asked Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) about a person who has never performed Hajj (ṣarūrah): "Can they perform Hajj using Zakat funds?"
The Imam (as) replied: "Yes."
records.arabicText: 1632 - وَ سَأَلَ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُسْلِمٍ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : عَنِ اَلصَّرُورَةِ أَ يَحُجُّ مِنَ اَلزَّكَاةِ قَالَ «نَعَمْ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.1632 - Muhammad ibn Muslim asked Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) about a person who has never performed Hajj (ṣarūrah): "Can they perform Hajj using Zakat funds?"
The Imam (as) replied:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Yes."

records.chapter: Chapter on Categories Upon Whom Zakat is Obligatory
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1633 - Ali ibn Yaqtin said to Abu al-Hasan al-Awwal (Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as)):
"I have Zakat funds in my possession. Can I use them to send my dependents and relatives to perform Hajj?"
The Imam (as) replied: "Yes, there is no issue."
records.arabicText: 1633 - وَ قَالَ عَلِيُّ بْنُ يَقْطِينٍ لِأَبِي اَلْحَسَنِ اَلْأَوَّلِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : يَكُونُ عِنْدِيَ اَلْمَالُ مِنَ اَلزَّكَاةِ فَأُحِجُّ بِهِ مَوَالِيَّ وَ أَقَارِبِي قَالَ «نَعَمْ لاَ بَأْسَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.1633 - Ali ibn Yaqtin said to Abu al-Hasan al-Awwal (Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as)):
"I have Zakat funds in my possession. Can I use them to send my dependents and relatives to perform Hajj?"
The Imam (as) replied:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Yes, there is no issue."

records.chapter: Chapter on Categories Upon Whom Zakat is Obligatory
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1634 - Abdullah ibn Sinan narrated from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) who said:
A man asked him in my presence about the wealth of a slave: "Is Zakat obligatory on it?"
The Imam (as) replied: "No, even if he possesses a million dirhams. Moreover, if he were in need, he would not be entitled to receive anything from Zakat."
records.arabicText: 1634 - وَ رَوَى عَبْدُ اَللَّهِ بْنُ سِنَانٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: سَأَلَهُ رَجُلٌ وَ أَنَا حَاضِرٌ عَنْ مَالِ اَلْمَمْلُوكِ أَ عَلَيْهِ زَكَاةٌ فَقَالَ «لاَ وَ لَوْ كَانَ لَهُ أَلْفُ أَلْفِ دِرْهَمٍ وَ لَوِ اِحْتَاجَ لَمْ يَكُنْ لَهُ مِنَ اَلزَّكَاةِ شَيْءٌ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1634 - Abdullah ibn Sinan narrated from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
A man asked him in my presence about the wealth of a slave: "Is Zakat obligatory on it?"
The Imam (as) replied: "No, even if he possesses a million dirhams. Moreover, if he were in need, he would not be entitled to receive anything from Zakat."

records.chapter: Chapter on Categories Upon Whom Zakat is Obligatory
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1635 - In another report from Abdullah ibn Sinan, he said:
I asked him (Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as)) "A slave has wealth in his possession. Is Zakat obligatory on it?"
The Imam (as) replied: "No."
I then asked: "Is it obligatory on his master?"
Imam (as) replied: "No, because it has not reached the master, and it does not belong to the slave."
records.arabicText: 1635 - وَ فِي خَبَرٍ آخَرَ عَنْ عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ بْنِ سِنَانٍ قَالَ: قُلْتُ لَهُ مَمْلُوكٌ فِي يَدِهِ مَالٌ أَ عَلَيْهِ زَكَاةٌ قَالَ «لاَ» قَالَ قُلْتُ فَعَلَى سَيِّدِهِ فَقَالَ «لاَ لِأَنَّهُ لَمْ يَصِلْ إِلَى اَلسَّيِّدِ وَ لَيْسَ هُوَ لِلْمَمْلُوكِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.1635 - In another report from Abdullah ibn Sinan, he said:
I asked him (Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as))
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"A slave has wealth in his possession. Is Zakat obligatory on it?"
The Imam (as) replied: "No."
I then asked: "Is it obligatory on his master?"
Imam (as) replied: "No, because it has not reached the master, and it does not belong to the slave."

records.chapter: Chapter on Categories Upon Whom Zakat is Obligatory
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1636 - In a narration from Wahb ibn Wahb al-Qurashi, from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) from his forefathers, from Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) who said:
"There is no Zakat on the wealth of a mukatib (a slave under a contract for emancipation)."
records.arabicText: 1636 - وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ وَهْبِ بْنِ وَهْبٍ اَلْقُرَشِيِّ عَنْ اَلصَّادِقِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنْ آبَائِهِ عَنْ عَلِيٍّ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: «لَيْسَ فِي مَالِ اَلْمُكَاتَبِ زَكَاةٌ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1636 - In a narration from Wahb ibn Wahb al-Qurashi, from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) from his forefathers, from Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "There is no Zakat on the wealth of a mukatib (a slave under a contract for emancipation)."

records.chapter: Chapter on Categories Upon Whom Zakat is Obligatory
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1637 - Abu Khadijah Salim ibn Mukrim al-Jammal narrated from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) who said:
"Give Zakat to those from Banu Hashim who seek it, for it is permissible for them. It is prohibited only for the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him and his family, for the Imam who succeeds him, and for the Imams, peace be upon them."
records.arabicText: 1637 - وَ رَوَى أَبُو خَدِيجَةَ سَالِمُ بْنُ مُكْرَمٍ اَلْجَمَّالُ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: «أَعْطُوا اَلزَّكَاةَ مَنْ أَرَادَهَا مِنْ بَنِي هَاشِمٍ فَإِنَّهَا تَحِلُّ لَهُمْ وَ إِنَّمَا تَحْرُمُ عَلَى اَلنَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ وَ عَلَى اَلْإِمَامِ اَلَّذِي بَعْدَهُ وَ عَلَى اَلْأَئِمَّةِ عَلَيْهِمُ اَلسَّلاَمُ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1637 - Abu Khadijah Salim ibn Mukrim al-Jammal narrated from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Give Zakat to those from Banu Hashim who seek it, for it is permissible for them. It is prohibited only for the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him and his family, for the Imam who succeeds him, and for the Imams, peace be upon them."

records.chapter: Chapter on Categories Upon Whom Zakat is Obligatory
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1638 - Al-Qasim ibn Sulayman narrated from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) who said:
"The charities of the Messenger of Allah (swt), peace and blessings be upon him and his family, and the charities of Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) are permissible for Banu Hashim."
records.arabicText: 1638 - وَ رَوَى اَلْقَاسِمُ بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: «إِنَّ صَدَقَاتِ رَسُولِ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ وَ صَدَقَاتِ عَلِيٍّ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ تَحِلُّ لِبَنِي هَاشِمٍ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1638 - Al-Qasim ibn Sulayman narrated from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The charities of the Messenger of Allah (swt), peace and blessings be upon him and his family, and the charities of Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) are permissible for Banu Hashim."

records.chapter: Chapter on Categories Upon Whom Zakat is Obligatory
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1639 - Al-Halabi narrated from him (Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as)):
"Fatimah {s.a}, peace be upon her, allocated her charities for Banu Hashim and Banu al-Muttalib."
records.arabicText: 1639 - وَ رَوَى اَلْحَلَبِيُّ عَنْهُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «أَنَّ فَاطِمَةَ عَلَيْهَا اَلسَّلاَمُ جَعَلَتْ صَدَقَاتِهَا لِبَنِي هَاشِمٍ وَ بَنِي اَلْمُطَّلِبِ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1639 - Al-Halabi narrated from him (Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as)):
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Fatimah {s.a}, peace be upon her, allocated her charities for Banu Hashim and Banu al-Muttalib."

records.chapter: Chapter on Categories Upon Whom Zakat is Obligatory
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1640 - Muhammad ibn Isma'il ibn Bazi’ narrated:
I sent some dinars to al-Ridha (Imam Ali ibn Musa al-Ridha (as)) on behalf of some of my family members. I wrote to him informing him that seventy-five of them were Zakat, and the remainder was a gift.
Imam (as) wrote back in his handwriting: "I have received them."
records.arabicText: 1640 - وَ رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ إِسْمَاعِيلَ بْنِ بَزِيعٍ قَالَ: بَعَثْتُ إِلَى اَلرِّضَا عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ بِدَنَانِيرَ مِنْ قِبَلِ بَعْضِ أَهْلِي وَ كَتَبْتُ إِلَيْهِ أُخْبِرُهُ أَنَّ فِيهَا زَكَاةً خَمْسَةٌ وَ سَبْعُونَ وَ اَلْبَاقِيَ صِلَةٌ فَكَتَبَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ بِخَطِّهِ «قَبَضْتُ» وَ بَعَثْتُ إِلَيْهِ بِدَنَانِيرَ لِي وَ لِغَيْرِي وَ كَتَبْتُ إِلَيْهِ أَنَّهَا مِنْ فِطْرَةِ اَلْعِيَالِ فَكَتَبَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ بِخَطِّهِ «قَبَضْتُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1640 - Muhammad ibn Isma'il ibn Bazi’ narrated:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
I sent some dinars to al-Ridha (Imam Ali ibn Musa al-Ridha (as)) on behalf of some of my family members. I wrote to him informing him that seventy-five of them were Zakat, and the remainder was a gift.
Imam (as) wrote back in his handwriting: "I have received them."

records.chapter: Chapter on Miscellaneous Issues Regarding Zakat
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1641 - It is narrated from Ali ibn Yaqtin, who said:
I said to Abu al-Hasan al-Awwal (Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as))
"A man died, and he had Zakat due on him. He instructed in his will that the Zakat be paid on his behalf, but his children are needy. Paying the Zakat would cause them severe hardship. What should they do?"
The Imam (as) replied: "They should pay the Zakat and then return it to themselves, keeping a portion to be given to others."
records.arabicText: 1641 - رُوِيَ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ يَقْطِينٍ قَالَ: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي اَلْحَسَنِ اَلْأَوَّلِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ رَجُلٌ مَاتَ وَ عَلَيْهِ زَكَاةٌ وَ أَوْصَى أَنْ تُقْضَى عَنْهُ اَلزَّكَاةُ وَ وُلْدُهُ مَحَاوِيجُ إِنْ دَفَعُوهَا أَضَرَّ بِهِمْ ذَلِكَ ضَرَراً شَدِيداً فَقَالَ «يُخْرِجُونَهَا فَيَعُودُونَ بِهَا عَلَى أَنْفُسِهِمْ وَ يُخْرِجُونَ مِنْهَا شَيْئاً فَيُدْفَعُ إِلَى غَيْرِهِمْ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.1641 - It is narrated from Ali ibn Yaqtin, who said:
I said to Abu al-Hasan al-Awwal (Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as))
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"A man died, and he had Zakat due on him. He instructed in his will that the Zakat be paid on his behalf, but his children are needy. Paying the Zakat would cause them severe hardship. What should they do?"
The Imam (as) replied: "They should pay the Zakat and then return it to themselves, keeping a portion to be given to others."

records.chapter: Chapter on Miscellaneous Issues Regarding Zakat
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1642 - Isma'il ibn Jabir narrated:
I said to Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) "Is it permissible for a man to take Zakat even though he does not need it, and then give it in charity?"
Imam (as) replied: "Yes."
Imam (as) also said the same regarding the Fitrah.
records.arabicText: 1642 - وَ رَوَى إِسْمَاعِيلُ بْنُ جَابِرٍ قَالَ: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ يَحِلُّ لِلرَّجُلِ أَنْ يَأْخُذَ اَلزَّكَاةَ وَ هُوَ لاَ يَحْتَاجُ إِلَيْهَا فَيَتَصَدَّقَ بِهَا قَالَ «نَعَمْ» وَ قَالَ فِي اَلْفِطْرَةِ مِثْلَ ذَلِكَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.1642 - Isma'il ibn Jabir narrated:
I said to Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) "Is it permissible for a man to take Zakat even though he does not need it, and then give it in charity?"
records.thaqalaynMatn:
Imam (as) replied: "Yes."
Imam (as) also said the same regarding the Fitrah.

records.chapter: Chapter on Miscellaneous Issues Regarding Zakat
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1643 - It is narrated from Abu Basir, who said:
I asked Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) "What obligation does the Imam have concerning Zakat?"
Imam (as) replied: "O Abu Muhammad, do you not know that the world belongs to the Imam? He places it wherever he wishes and gives it to whomever he wishes, and this is permissible for him by Allah (swt), the Almighty. The Imam never spends a single night while there remains a right of Allah (swt), the Almighty, upon him without fulfilling it, for Allah (swt) will hold him accountable for it."
records.arabicText: 1643 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ قَالَ: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ مَا عَلَى اَلْإِمَامِ مِنَ اَلزَّكَاةِ فَقَالَ «يَا أَبَا مُحَمَّدٍ أَ مَا عَلِمْتَ أَنَّ اَلدُّنْيَا لِلْإِمَامِ يَضَعُهَا حَيْثُ يَشَاءُ وَ يَدْفَعُهَا إِلَى مَنْ يَشَاءُ جَائِزٌ مِنَ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ لَهُ ذَلِكَ إِنَّ اَلْإِمَامَ لاَ يَبِيتُ لَيْلَةً أَبَداً وَ لِلَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ فِي عُنُقِهِ حَقٌّ يَسْأَلُهُ عَنْهُ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.1643 - It is narrated from Abu Basir, who said:
I asked Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) "What obligation does the Imam have concerning Zakat?"
Imam (as) replied:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "O Abu Muhammad, do you not know that the world belongs to the Imam? He places it wherever he wishes and gives it to whomever he wishes, and this is permissible for him by Allah (swt), the Almighty. The Imam never spends a single night while there remains a right of Allah (swt), the Almighty, upon him without fulfilling it, for Allah (swt) will hold him accountable for it."

records.chapter: Chapter on Khums
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1644 - Abu al-Hasan Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as) was asked about what is extracted from the sea, such as pearls, rubies, and emeralds, and about mines of gold and silver: "Is there Zakat on them?"
Imam (as) replied: "If their value reaches one dinar, then Khums (one-fifth) is due on them."
records.arabicText: 1644 - سُئِلَ أَبُو اَلْحَسَنِ مُوسَى بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ : عَمَّا يُخْرَجُ مِنَ اَلْبَحْرِ مِنَ اَللُّؤْلُؤِ وَ اَلْيَاقُوتِ وَ اَلزَّبَرْجَدِ وَ عَنْ مَعَادِنِ اَلذَّهَبِ وَ اَلْفِضَّةِ هَلْ فِيهَا زَكَاةٌ فَقَالَ «إِذَا بَلَغَ قِيمَتُهُ دِينَاراً فَفِيهِ اَلْخُمُسُ ».

records.chapter: Chapter on Khums
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1645 - Ubaydullah ibn Ali al-Halabi asked Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) about buried treasure: "How much is due on it?"
Imam (as) replied: "The Khums (one-fifth)."
He then asked about mines: "How much is due on them?"
Imam (as) replied: "The Khums."
He further asked about lead, brass, iron, and other mineral types: "How much is due on them?"
The Imam (as) replied: "The same is taken from them as is taken from mines of gold and silver."
records.arabicText: 1645 - وَ سَأَلَ عُبَيْدُ اَللَّهِ بْنُ عَلِيٍّ اَلْحَلَبِيُّ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : عَنِ اَلْكَنْزِ كَمْ فِيهِ فَقَالَ «اَلْخُمُسُ» وَ عَنِ اَلْمَعَادِنِ كَمْ فِيهَا فَقَالَ «اَلْخُمُسُ» وَ عَنِ اَلرَّصَاصِ وَ اَلصُّفْرِ وَ اَلْحَدِيدِ وَ مَا كَانَ مِنَ اَلْمَعَادِنِ كَمْ فِيهَا فَقَالَ «يُؤْخَذُ مِنْهَا كَمَا يُؤْخَذُ مِنْ مَعَادِنِ اَلذَّهَبِ وَ اَلْفِضَّةِ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.1645 - Ubaydullah ibn Ali al-Halabi asked Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) about buried treasure: "How much is due on it?"
Imam (as) replied:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"The Khums (one-fifth)."
He then asked about mines: "How much is due on them?"
Imam (as) replied: "The Khums."
He further asked about lead, brass, iron, and other mineral types: "How much is due on them?"
The Imam (as) replied: "The same is taken from them as is taken from mines of gold and silver."

records.chapter: Chapter on Khums
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1646 - Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Abdullah ibn Sinan, who said:
I heard Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) say:
"Khums is only applicable to spoils of war specifically."
records.arabicText: 1646 - وَ رَوَى اَلْحَسَنُ بْنُ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ بْنِ سِنَانٍ قَالَ سَمِعْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ يَقُولُ: «لَيْسَ اَلْخُمُسُ إِلاَّ فِي اَلْغَنَائِمِ خَاصَّةً ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.1646 - Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Abdullah ibn Sinan, who said:
I heard Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) say:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Khums is only applicable to spoils of war specifically."
